@Yannizo. from an art student perspective it's really good LOL, you have good reasonings, I'd just like to put mine as well!
- the use of negative space behind the trees
- the focal point is very clear with the shimmer coming out of the book & the way the tree branches curve inward & the edge of the letters frame the figures
- the use of analogous colors w/ the red as a complementary shade to really make the colors all pop
- I cant speak on typography since that's not what I do, so that's all you
-there's a lot of perspective & I love how the comic style looks, the books I used to read as a kid sometimes had pictures in them like this, so in my perspective it's as if the nostalgia is coming to life
- Harsh black shading works well here, it gives more of a horror tone that a typical portrait drawing
- the slight foreshortening of the characters is really well done
-lighting was done really well, you can tell where the light source is coming from & how it tones the characters!
​@Yannizo.at first I disagreed with you  I still think it's a great album cover but I do agree the cartooney look doesn't go along with the tone of the album

​@Squiddle Beans Eh, to each their own I guess and if you like it that's totally cool, art is always subjective. But there are some typical "artistic sins" put into this cover art. First of all, I'm a graphic designer, I went to an art school and I'm comfortable saying I know some things about esthetic composition. Of course that doesn't mean that my opinion is in any way superior to others, but I'd like to elaborate my thoughts on it:

- It's way too much: The term "horror vacui" fits it pretty well. The illustration in the front, the typefaces, the trees in the background, the moon, the blood, the flourish on the edges, etc. The designer must have feared the concept of putting an empty space somewhere.

- The illustrations are bad: It's too cartoony, doesn't fit the tone of the album, and it kinda seems as if someone made it who has been a hobby artist for a few weeks.

- Bad use of color theory: The synyster green combined with the harsh red isn't pleasing to the eye.

- Bad shading: The characters and also the trees in the back are shaded with a soft 100% black shadow. This always looks cheap.

- Bad typeface: The text on the bottom is a typological mess. It's too big of a contrast to the bands logo. Also, never put a random outline to a font.

- Bad effects: The blood drips as well as the lens flare coming from the middle of the book and the fog effects look like some png picture they found on Google images.

Overall I think it's some pretty bad cover illustration. Personally it's one of my favorite albums but I can't deny this cover looks horrible. 
However, I respect your opinion of course :)
